questions.question: Explain the role of SQLiteOpenHelper for database creation and connection. Describe its key methods.
keyPoints: SQLiteOpenHelper is an abstract helper class that manages database creation and version management.
onCreate(SQLiteDatabase db) is called when the database is created for the first time.
onUpgrade(SQLiteDatabase db, int oldVersion, int newVersion) is called for database upgrades.
getWritableDatabase() and getReadableDatabase() open or create a database connection.

questions.question: What is a database transaction in SQLite and why is it important? Provide a code example.
keyPoints: A transaction groups database operations into a single atomic unit of work.
Transactions maintain data integrity and consistency.
Transactions improve performance by reducing disk writes.
Example: db.beginTransaction(); try { db.setTransactionSuccessful(); } finally { db.endTransaction(); }

questions.question: List the two primary methods to obtain a connection to an SQLite database and state the key difference.
keyPoints: getWritableDatabase() opens for both reading and writing.
getReadableDatabase() may return read-only in exceptional situations like disk full.
getWritableDatabase() throws SQLiteException if it cannot open for writing.
